urgent_methodology: Publisher-marked Breaking, Urgent, Alert, Just in, Developing or similar items are collected first and receive priority over standard items, unless they are liveblogs, programme previews or low-value content.

latest.title: More than 2,700 people may have died in exceptional May and June heatwaves in England and Wales
latest.summary: An estimated 2,700 people may have died in England and Wales due to extreme heat during May and June, according to a study by Imperial College London, the Met Office, and the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. The June heatwave, which saw record temperatures of 37.7°C in Norfolk, was the warmest June on record in England.
latest.details: An estimated 2,700 people may have died in England and Wales due to extreme heat during May and June, according to a study by Imperial College London, the Met Office, and the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. The June heatwave, which saw record temperatures of 37.7°C in Norfolk, was the warmest June on record in England. Researchers attribute the heatwaves to a "heat dome" exacerbated by human-induced climate change, which added 3-4°C to the maximum temperatures. Vulnerable groups, such as the elderly and those with pre-existing health conditions, were particularly at risk, and the heat was described as a "silent killer" due to its subtle early symptoms.
latest.full_brief: An estimated 2,700 people may have died in England and Wales due to extreme heat during May and June, according to a study by Imperial College London, the Met Office, and the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. The June heatwave, which saw record temperatures of 37.7°C in Norfolk, was the warmest June on record in England. Researchers attribute the heatwaves to a "heat dome" exacerbated by human-induced climate change, which added 3-4°C to the maximum temperatures. Vulnerable groups, such as the elderly and those with pre-existing health conditions, were particularly at risk, and the heat was described as a "silent killer" due to its subtle early symptoms.

latest.title: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops
latest.summary: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement.
latest.details: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement. The arrested individuals, aged between 30 and 59, are accused of violating national security laws and could face up to seven years in prison. Amnesty International criticized the raids, stating they reflect a climate of fear and self-censorship in Hong Kong.
latest.full_brief: Five people were arrested in Hong Kong for selling books deemed 'seditious' by authorities. Police raided two independent bookshops, seizing publications that allegedly incited hatred against the government, judiciary, and law enforcement. The arrested individuals, aged between 30 and 59, are accused of violating national security laws and could face up to seven years in prison. Amnesty International criticized the raids, stating they reflect a climate of fear and self-censorship in Hong Kong.

latest.title: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
latest.summary: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion.
latest.details: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion. Koroma expressed gratitude to President Julius Maada Bio, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu, and Ecowas for their support during the process. The attempted coup in November 2023 resulted in 20 deaths and the release of nearly 2,000 inmates, with 35 individuals later convicted and sentenced to long prison terms.
latest.full_brief: Sierra Leone's government has dropped treason charges against ex-President Ernest Bai Koroma, who was accused of involvement in a 2023 attempted coup. Information Minister Chernor Bah stated the charges were dropped on health grounds, allowing Koroma to return to Sierra Leone at his discretion. Koroma expressed gratitude to President Julius Maada Bio, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu, and Ecowas for their support during the process. The attempted coup in November 2023 resulted in 20 deaths and the release of nearly 2,000 inmates, with 35 individuals later convicted and sentenced to long prison terms.

latest.title: Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators
latest.summary: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on last year's Air India crash could be ready in October. The crash occurred on June 12, 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, killing 260 people.
latest.details: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on last year's Air India crash could be ready in October. The crash occurred on June 12, 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, killing 260 people. The AAIB has completed 49 of the 66 mandated steps for investigating serious aircraft accidents, including analyzing flight recorders and conducting a psychological autopsy. A preliminary report suggested that fuel-control switches moved to the "cut-off" position after takeoff, causing total engine power loss, but the cause remains under investigation.
latest.full_brief: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on last year's Air India crash could be ready in October. The crash occurred on June 12, 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, killing 260 people. The AAIB has completed 49 of the 66 mandated steps for investigating serious aircraft accidents, including analyzing flight recorders and conducting a psychological autopsy. A preliminary report suggested that fuel-control switches moved to the "cut-off" position after takeoff, causing total engine power loss, but the cause remains under investigation.

latest.title: Australia news live: Josh Thomas weighs in on Divine Playhouse debate; meth surges in workplace…
latest.summary: The Australian government has announced plans to strengthen modern slavery laws, targeting companies with revenue over $100 million that fail to prevent modern slavery in their supply chains. Attorney General Michelle Rowland stated that the government will consult on the details and provide a defense for companies that can demonstrate reasonable steps to prevent modern slavery.
latest.details: The Australian government has announced plans to strengthen modern slavery laws, targeting companies with revenue over $100 million that fail to prevent modern slavery in their supply chains. Attorney General Michelle Rowland stated that the government will consult on the details and provide a defense for companies that can demonstrate reasonable steps to prevent modern slavery. The government also plans to introduce civil penalties for non-compliance under the existing modern slavery act. Additionally, seven new members were appointed to the modern slavery expert advisory group to support these efforts.
latest.full_brief: The Australian government has announced plans to strengthen modern slavery laws, targeting companies with revenue over $100 million that fail to prevent modern slavery in their supply chains. Attorney General Michelle Rowland stated that the government will consult on the details and provide a defense for companies that can demonstrate reasonable steps to prevent modern slavery. The government also plans to introduce civil penalties for non-compliance under the existing modern slavery act. Additionally, seven new members were appointed to the modern slavery expert advisory group to support these efforts.

brief_methodology.pl: Komentarz powstaje wyłącznie z przeczytanego tekstu artykułu. Musi przejść niezależny przegląd AI oraz pełną kontrolę językową; surowy tekst artykułu lub RSS nie może być komentarzem awaryjnym.
brief_methodology.en: A comment is created only from the retrieved article text. It must pass an independent AI review and the complete language gate; raw article or RSS text is never a fallback comment.

comment_quality_gate.pl: Publikowany jest wyłącznie komentarz utworzony z tekstu artykułu, zatwierdzony w niezależnym przeglądzie AI i przyjęty w całości przez rygorystyczną kontrolę językową. Jedno wadliwe zdanie odrzuca cały komentarz.
comment_quality_gate.en: Only an article-derived comment approved by an independent AI review and accepted in full by the strict language gate is published. One defective sentence rejects the whole comment.

rejected_examples.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says

same_story_dedupe.rule: One underlying event gets one homepage card, even if multiple publishers cover it with different links.
